name: theme flip via single token-inversion class
description: The Lotus Seat site themed itself by putting a .stage class on <body> in layout.tsx, which re-pointed every --color-* token to a dark set; all components use semantic utilities (bg-paper/text-ink), so the whole site inverts from one class. To go light: drop .stage from body and delete the block. Gotchas that do NOT follow automatically: (1) sections painted bg-graphite (Footer, Materials, Lifestyle) assumed inverted text and go dark-on-dark, (2) chips over photographs need their own contrast (bg-paper/90 text-ink), not the section's, (3) text-cork-bright fails contrast on paper — use text-cork-deep, (4) the Three.js scene in cushionScene.ts hardcodes scene.background, a 0xffffff wireframe, and dark-stage lighting (low hemisphere, strong key + rim) — on paper it needs a graphite wireframe, ~2x hemisphere bounce, softer key, near-zero rim.

Next.js client components ('use client') cannot export const metadata — the app/faq page and app/contact page both hit this. Also: a root layout title.template of '%s | Brand' silently double-brands any page whose own title already ends in '— Brand'.
resolution: For a client page, add a sibling route layout.tsx (app/contact/layout.tsx) that exports metadata and returns children. Or split the page into a server page.tsx + a client child holding the state. When introducing a title template, grep all existing 'title:' strings and strip the old brand suffix.

name: mobile-only sticky CTA — avoid duplicating the desktop nav
description: src/components/StickyPreorderBar.tsx: persistent buy CTA mounted in the root layout. Deliberately md:hidden — the fixed Navbar already carries a Pre-Order button on desktop, but on mobile that CTA is buried inside the collapsed hamburger, so after the hero scrolls away there is NO visible way to buy. Appears past window.innerHeight * 0.85 so it never competes with the hero's own CTA. Uses inert (not just translate-y-full) while off-screen so keyboard users cannot tab into an invisible button, and returns null on /checkout* via usePathname. Price is resolved server-side in layout.tsx from priceFor('EUR') and passed as a prop, so the bar cannot quote a number the cart disagrees with.
